title: Mellow Filipino Peanut Tagine
description: A solo dinner Filipino dish featuring peanut cookies in a silky bitter sauce, cooked in a ceramic tagine using a bain-marie method, suitable for shojin-friendly diets.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a blender or food processor, blend the crushed peanut cookies into a fine crumb.
In a large ceramic tagine, heat the vegetable oil over low heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for an additional 1 minute.
In a small bowl, whisk together the coconut milk, soy sauce, and maple syrup.
Pour the coconut milk mixture into the tagine and stir to combine.
Add the blended peanut cookies, black pepper, and salt to the tagine and stir to combine.
Cover the tagine and place it in a larger dish or tray filled with water to create a bain-marie.
Bake for 25 minutes or until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
